title: SpaceX will join Nasdaq-100
summary: Adding SpaceX this quickly would make the Elon Musk company one of the first beneficiaries of Nasdaq's recently adopted fast-track inclusion framework.

title: What will Europe’s heatwave cost?
summary: Growing body of research points to economic damage from extreme heat

title: SpaceX plans Starlink mobile push into US consumer market
summary: Move would test whether group can turn sky-high ambition into a mass-market phone business

title: 1 year after launch, spaceship shooter Wildgate will end development after its next update
summary: Thankfully, it's not shutting down, and developer Moonshot Games says no layoffs are planned.
body: After a year of struggling to pull in players, the PvPvE game Wildgate is calling it quits. Game director Dustin Browder said in a Steam update posted today that despite the best efforts of developer Moonshot Games, "Wildgate hasn’t found a large enough audience to sustain ongoing development," and so it's ending further updates. "Wildgate is not going away," Browder wrote. "Wildgate will continue to receive occasional updates like bug fixes, balance updates, and small events, activations or rewards that generally repurpose existing content. And new players will still be able to buy the game on all platforms We're keeping track of all the Amazon Prime Day PC gaming deals here (Steam, EGS, Xbox, and PS)—and we plan on participating in as many sales and discounts as we can." Despite landing with unmistakable finality, Browder appeared to leave the door open for a comeback, writing that the new patch coming in July will be the game's "last big patch for a while." The update also aims to ensure "you can keep playing," with changes including additional information on game modes, additional player-controlled options for custom modes, new sprays and items in the in-game store, and a rang…
